steps.title: Identify the contrast
steps.content: The first sentence says the volcanoes “may look similar from afar.” The second immediately pivots: volcanologists “can tell...that” they are actually different types. The key signal is the contradiction between what we observe (appearance-based similarity) and what experts know (difference in formation).

steps.title: Apply the contrast
steps.content: ”Though” is a contrast word that captures this exact shift: despite looking similar, volcanologists recognize they are fundamentally different. The other three either add without contrasting (“in addition”), suggest false causation (“therefore”), or introduce an unrelated example (“for example”). This matches choice D — D is correct.
